seo.title: Building AI Email Assistant with OpenAI Agents SDK + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build ai email assistant with OpenAI Agents SDK + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.
keywords: OpenAI Agents SDK ai email assistant

content.intro: Building an AI Email Assistant with OpenAI's Agents SDK enables sophisticated multi-agent workflows for intent detection, draft generation, attachment handling, and send approval. However, production email systems face high risks like PII leaks, inappropriate tone, and unauthorized sends due to agent handoff loops and unvalidated tool outputs. Vex provides essential output guardrails to verify content across all agents, ensuring reliability and compliance.

architectureOverview.heading: AI Email Assistant Architecture with OpenAI Agents SDK
architectureOverview.description:
The AI Email Assistant uses OpenAI Agents SDK to orchestrate specialized agents: an IntentDetectorAgent classifies email purpose (reply, forward, draft), a DraftGeneratorAgent creates responses with tone control, an AttachmentHandlerAgent processes files securely, and an ApprovalAgent validates before sending. Agents handoff via the SDK's agent.step() mechanism with shared context. Tracing spans multi-agent chains, but gaps expose risks in production.

Email flows through agents sequentially: incoming message → intent detection → draft generation → attachment validation → approval check → send or reject. Each agent uses tools for specific tasks (e.g., PII scanning, recipient lookup) and produces structured outputs consumed by downstream agents. Vex integrates as output parsers on each agent.step() to validate before handoff.

This modular design scales but amplifies failure modes like infinite loops between DraftGenerator and ApprovalAgent or guardrail bypasses via injected prompts in email content.
architectureOverview.diagram:
Incoming Email
       ↓
[IntentDetectorAgent] → intent: 'reply' | 'draft' | 'forward'
       ↓ (Vex Guardrail)
[DraftGeneratorAgent] → email draft + tone score
       ↓ (Vex Guardrail)
[AttachmentHandlerAgent] → safe attachments
       ↓ (Vex Guardrail)
[ApprovalAgent] → send_approved: true/false
       ↓
Send Email / Reject

risks.name: Agent handoff loops
risks.description: DraftGenerator and ApprovalAgent ping-pong indefinitely when tone validation fails repeatedly
risks.mitigation: Vex max_iterations guardrail with loop detection on agent outputs

risks.name: Guardrail bypass via prompt injection
risks.description: Malicious email content like 'ignore safety checks' tricks agents into bypassing PII filters
risks.mitigation: Vex input sanitization and output verification before agent consumption

risks.name: Unvalidated tool outputs
risks.description: AttachmentHandler returns unsafe file paths that ApprovalAgent sends without checking
risks.mitigation: Vex schema validation on all tool results before agent handoff

risks.name: PII leakage in drafts
risks.description: DraftGenerator includes unfiltered customer data from intent context
risks.mitigation: Vex PII detection guardrail on every agent output

implementation.title: Intent Detection Agent
implementation.description: Classifies email intent using Runner and specialized tools for categorization
implementation.code:
from agents import Agent, Runner, tool
from typing import Literal

@tool
def classify_intent(email_content: str) -> Literal['reply', 'draft', 'forward', 'schedule']:
    """Classify email intent"""
    # OpenAI model call logic here
    return 'reply'

intent_agent = Agent(
    name="Intent Detector",
    instructions="Classify email purpose accurately",
    tools=[classify_intent]
)

runner = Runner()
result = runner.run_sync(intent_agent, "Process: " + email_body)
intent = result.final_output

implementation.title: Draft Generation Agent
implementation.description: Generates email drafts with tone control based on intent handoff
implementation.code:
from agents import Agent, tool

@tool
def generate_draft(intent: str, original_email: str, tone: str = 'professional') -> str:
    """Generate email draft with specified tone"""
    # LLM generation logic
    return f"Dear {recipient},\n{response_content}"

draft_agent = Agent(
    name="Draft Generator",
    instructions="Generate appropriate email drafts. Maintain professional tone.",
    tools=[generate_draft]
)

result = runner.run_sync(draft_agent, f"Intent: {intent}, Email: {original_email}")
draft = result.final_output

vexIntegration.title: Vex Output Guardrail on Agent Steps
vexIntegration.description: Wrap every agent.run_sync() with Vex verify_output to catch failures before handoff

vexIntegration.title: PII Filtering Guardrail
vexIntegration.description: Blocks PII in all agent outputs using Vex's built-in detectors

vexIntegration.title: Loop Prevention with Vex Tracing
vexIntegration.description: Detects agent handoff loops and enforces max iterations with full tracing
vexIntegration.code:
from vex import Vex

vex = Vex(api_key="your-vex-key", trace=True)

class SafeRunner:
    def __init__(self):
        self.iteration_count = 0
        self.max_iterations = 5
    
    def run_with_safeguards(self, agent, input):
        if self.iteration_count >= self.max_iterations:
            raise Exception("Max iterations exceeded - potential loop")
        
        with vex.span(f"agent-{agent.name}"):
            result = runner.run_sync(agent, input)
            verified = vex.verify_output(result.final_output)
            
            self.iteration_count += 1
            return verified.output if verified.valid else None

safe_runner = SafeRunner()
result = safe_runner.run_with_safeguards(draft_agent, agent_input)
